persona.label: Software architect presenting frequently
persona.description: Owns architecture reviews, design proposals, and recurring technical briefings.
persona.surface_preference: deck-as-code authoring with quick local preview

persona_lens.starting_surface: Open the generated deck and inspect the JSON source before rendering video.
persona_lens.first_question: Can I turn an architecture decision into a polished, rerunnable presentation without hand-editing pixels?
persona_lens.evidence_emphasis: Validated Slidey JSON, playback clips, layout checks, and a bundle suitable for sharing.
persona_lens.escalation_trigger: Any visual that cannot be reproduced, diffed, bundled, or reviewed before a meeting.
persona_lens.finding_bias: Prefer findings that improve repeatable technical communication over one-off polish.

scenarios.label: Compose an architecture deck
scenarios.task: Turn a design decision into a deterministic architecture deck with diagrams, evidence, and speaker-ready narrative.
success_criteria: The deck source is understandable as code.
The resulting artifact can be bundled and shared.
The recorded authoring flow is replayable.

scenarios.label: Reuse a recorded demo
scenarios.task: Embed a recorded walkthrough so an audience can inspect the UI state without rerunning the system.
success_criteria: The deck contains a standalone playback scene.
The media path is deck-local and bundle-friendly.

scenarios.label: Maintain deck as code
scenarios.task: Review whether the JSON deck is diffable, deterministic, and maintainable for recurring technical presentations.
success_criteria: A reviewer can tell which run produced the deck.
The media contract is explicit enough to keep assets in sync.
